channel.title: SevenAtSevenPolitics
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenPolitics, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Courts Won’t Stop VA Dems’ Gerrymandering Gambit, So Where Is The GOP’s Ground Game?
item.description: Republicans cannot use lawsuits as their only strategy to stop gerrymandering. They need be in campaign mode, knocking doors, and getting people to th...

item.title: The Biblical Whirlwind of Death That Is About to Rain Down on the Mullahs
item.description: ‘I don’t think anyone really understands the scale or capacity we have because no one’s ever seen it before.’

item.title: Trump rips SCOTUS tariffs ruling, says he'll use other authorities to impose 10% global tariff
item.description: President Trump said Friday he is deeply disappointed by the Supreme Court's decision to strike down a major swath of his tariff plan, saying it would...

item.title: Montgomery County agrees to pay parents who sued to stop LGBT school lessons
item.description: Montgomery County in Maryland has agreed to pay the parents who had to sue to force the schools to allow their kids to opt out of pro-LGBT lessons.

item.title: Religious Freedom Is Syria’s First Test
item.description: Syria’s future depends on whether it will protect its most vulnerable citizens — Christians and Kurds.

item.title: Meet ‘Your Rich BFF,’ the former JPMorgan trader and TikTok star who wants you to talk about money on the first date
item.description: Vivian Tu, who refers to herself as “your favorite Wall Street girly,” also hosts the “Networth and Chill" podcast and published a book, “Well Endowed...

item.title: America Slides Into A Congress-Shaped Hole, And Gorsuch Helps
item.description: Our discussion, in media and in law, has been about Trump's excesses and boundary-crossing. A better starting place would be the laziness and uselessn...

item.title: Kavanaugh: Trump Obviously Has Tariff Power And Here’s How He Can Keep Using It
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court may have ruled against President Trump’s use of an emergency economics law to impose tariffs on foreign countries, but that doe...

item.title: Wall Street shrugs at widely expected Supreme Court tariff ruling
item.description: The S&P 500 was 0.1% higher in midday trading.

item.title: U.S. firms scramble for refunds after Supreme Court rejects Trump's blanket tariffs
item.description: The Supreme Court's decision to strike down Mr. Trump's use of the International Emergency Economic Powers Act is unleashing a scramble by firms to se...

item.title: Anna Murdoch-Mann, author and philanthropist, dead at 81
item.description: Anna Murdoch-Mann, Rupert Murdoch's ex-wife and News Corp partner, dies at 81. The philanthropist devoted her life to children's causes and charity wo...

item.title: Melania Trump to be immortalized in Smithsonian's First Ladies Collection
item.description: First lady Melania Trump presented her 2025 inaugural gown to the Smithsonian's National Museum of American History on Friday -- the first first lady ...

item.title: Trump gives grudging praise to liberal trio who helped sink his tariffs
item.description: Supreme Court rules 6-3 that President Donald Trump exceeded authority using emergency powers for tariffs, with conservative justices dissenting from ...

item.title: The Week: Mr. Rubio Goes to Munich
item.description: Plus: Zohran Mamdani’s budget blunder.

item.title: New York nurses union wins 12% raise, AI safeguards in a tentative deal to end monthlong strike
item.description: Over the past month, nurses had picketed during bitterly cold temperatures in what their union said was the largest and longest walkout in the city’s ...

item.title: What to know about the Florida bill to rename Palm Beach International Airport for Trump
item.description: Florida lawmakers this week approved renaming the international airport in West Palm Beach after President Donald Trump, just days after the Trump fam...

item.title: CFO whose business fell 10% from tariffs cheers Supreme Court ruling: ‘No single person should have unilateral authority over tariffs’
item.description: Matt Totsch, the CFO of manufacturer Trim-Tex, said businesses like his have been dealing with crippling uncertainty caused by Trump’s chaotic tariff ...

item.title: Supreme Court Rules Against Trump In Emergency Tariffs Cases
item.description: '[The International Emergency Economic Powers Act] IEEPA does not authorize the President to impose tariffs,' the court ruled.

item.title: Collateral Damage for the Coal Industry?
item.description: A lawsuit over ESG policies could have unintended consequences.

item.title: How Jeffrey Epstein exploited colleges’ ‘tremendous drive to acquire money’
item.description: Schools try to make sure there is no financial conflict of interest for the donor, researcher or institution. Emphasis on the "try."

item.title: Omar calls GOP probe into husband’s $30M business surge a ‘political stunt’ as records deadline passes
item.description: Rep. Ilhan Omar called GOP investigation into her finances a "political stunt" as the deadline to provide business records about husband's companies b...

item.title: What Would George Washington Do?
item.description: Adhering to Washington’s ‘Rules of Civility’ would probably solve a good deal of our cultural problems.

item.title: 4 Of The Best Lines From Kavanaugh’s Masterclass Defense Of Trump’s Tariff Power
item.description: 'As they interpret the statute, the President could, for example, block all imports from China but cannot order even a $1 tariff on goods imported fro...

item.title: Fox News Poll: Trump’s tariffs faced broad disapproval even before Supreme Court ruling
item.description: New Fox News poll shows 63% of voters disapprove of Trump's tariff handling despite record revenue surge of over 300% since taking office this year.

item.title: Democrats cheer Supreme Court move blocking Trump tariffs — despite past support for trade duties
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries and Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer led Democrats in praising the Supreme Court on Friday for striking down...

item.title: Why Static Portfolios Fail When Risk Regimes Change
item.description: Why static portfolio frameworks fail when risk regimes shift, drawing lessons from the very different market breakdowns of 2020 and 2022. The post Why...

item.title: What the Market Knows That WACC Doesn’t
item.description: Market-implied discount rates reveal how investors price risk, often diverging from WACC and reshaping capital decisions. The post What the Market Kno...

item.title: Attention Bias in AI-Driven Investing
item.description: AI tools may favor popular stocks over overlooked ones, embedding attention bias into investment decisions. The post Attention Bias in AI-Driven Inves...

item.title: Social Security Claiming Strategies for High-Net-Worth Clients
item.description: Social Security claiming is a capital-allocation decision for affluent clients. This analysis weighs taxes, longevity risk, and liquidity trade-offs. ...

item.title: Evolving Your Wealth Management Practice for 2026 and Beyond
item.description: Wealth management’s 2026 outlook: growth will hinge on transparency, integration, and relevance to women and next-generation investors. The post Evolv...
